contexts.context: ...same penetrating spirit. Many well-meaning enthusiastick <mark>criticks</mark> have given themselves a great deal of trouble in...
...same time; and experience has taught us, that <mark>criticks</mark> do not unfrequently write in contradiction to their own...
...volume should appear, to any candid and liberal <mark>critick</mark>, to merit that he should take the trouble...
...who, perhaps, ought only to have been our <mark>Criticks</mark> have become our Poets. I mean not, however...
